description: Issuer eligibility rules (S3): application blocks, bonus ineligibility, and yearly bonus capacity from spend velocity

scenarios.name: Eligibility - Chase 5/24 blocks new Chase card
scenarios.description: Five personal cards opened within 24 months: the Chase candidate must not be recommended even though its bonus is attractive. Uses opened_days_ago so the window never goes stale.

test_conditions: 5 personal cards opened in the last 24 months trip Chase 5/24
chase-test-rewards is silently excluded from apply recommendations

scenarios.name: Eligibility - Under 5/24 allows the Chase card
scenarios.description: Same profile but two of the five cards were opened >24 months ago, so the user is at 3/24 and the Chase card is recommendable.

test_conditions: Only 3 cards opened within 24 months — 5/24 does not trip
The 2% Chase card beats the owned 1x cards for general spending

scenarios.name: Eligibility - Amex lifetime rule zeroes the bonus
scenarios.description: User held (and closed) the Amex card years ago. It's still worth applying for the 3x dining, but the once-per-lifetime rule values the signup bonus at $0.

test_conditions: Closed card still counts as prior ownership for Amex lifetime rule
Bonus valued at $0; card recommended on ongoing 3x dining value alone

scenarios.name: Bonus capacity - only two 10K bonuses fit a year
scenarios.description: A $2,000/month spender is offered three cards that each need $10,000 of spending (~5 months each). Only two bonuses fit in 12 months of spending; the third card is deferred even though max_recommendations allows it.

test_conditions: Each $10K requirement consumes ~5 months of the $2K/mo spending
5 + 5 = 10 months fits; the third bonus (15 months) is deferred
max_recommendations=3 proves the cap comes from spend velocity, not the count
